habitat: Breeds in marshy woods and tundra; winters in marshes; estuaries; freshwater lakes; and rivers
story: The Baikai Teal (Sibirionetta formosa) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Anatidae family. It is most commonly observed in Breeds in marshy woods and tundra; winters in marshes; estuaries; freshwater lakes; and rivers, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Least Concern (LC) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Baikai Teal is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Baikai Teal into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
